news.headline: Tenax Therapeutics (TENX) Gets Updated 3.3% Ownership Filing From Millennium Group
news.summary: Millennium Management and affiliated funds (including Israel A. Englander) filed an amended Schedule 13G/A disclosing a reduced 3.3% passive stake in Tenax Therapeutics, down from the 5.1% stake reported in May 2026. The trim came in the run-up to the Phase 3 LEVEL data readout expected in August 2026.

news.headline: Tenax Therapeutics, Inc. Receives Consensus Recommendation of "Moderate Buy" from Analysts
news.summary: Analysts covering TENX give it a consensus "Moderate Buy"/"Strong Buy" rating with average 12-month price targets in the mid-$30s, following recent target hikes from Piper Sandler (to $50, June 4) and Guggenheim (to $40, June 22), underscoring bullish sell-side positioning heading into the Phase 3 LEVEL data readout.

news.headline: Short Interest in Tenax Therapeutics (TENX) Increases by 132.9%
news.summary: Short interest in TENX jumped 132.9% to 1.79 million shares (about 7.0% of float) as of June 30, 2026, up from 767,532 shares two weeks earlier, signaling a sharp rise in bearish or hedging positioning ahead of the binary Phase 3 LEVEL data readout.

news.headline: FMR LLC (Fidelity) Discloses New 14.9% Stake in Tenax Therapeutics
news.summary: FMR LLC, the parent of Fidelity Management & Research, disclosed a new passive stake of 3,942,373 shares (14.9%) in Tenax Therapeutics as of June 30, 2026, one of the largest institutional accumulations disclosed ahead of the LEVEL trial readout. T. Rowe Price separately reported an even larger 17.2% stake in a related 13G/A filing.

news.headline: Tenax Therapeutics Announces Late-Breaking Presentation of LEVEL Clinical Trial Data at ESC Congress 2026
news.summary: Tenax will present Phase 3 LEVEL trial results for oral levosimendan (TNX-103) in a Late-Breaking Clinical Science session at ESC Congress 2026 in Munich on August 29, with topline data now expected in August 2026 ahead of database lock and statistical analysis.

news.headline: Tenax and Orion Secure Long-Term Levosimendan Supply Deal
news.summary: Tenax and Orion Corporation signed a five-year, auto-renewing supply agreement naming Orion the primary manufacturer of oral levosimendan (TNX-103), alongside a license amendment extending the deadline for U.S. regulatory approval to December 31, 2035.

catalysts.label: Phase 3 LEVEL topline data readout
catalysts.detail: Tenax expects to report topline data from the registrational Phase 3 LEVEL trial of oral levosimendan (TNX-103) in PH-HFpEF in August 2026; enrollment (230+ patients) completed in March 2026 and the trial has since moved through database lock and statistical analysis. The trial is powered at well over 90% to detect a 25-meter change in 6-minute walk distance, its primary endpoint.

catalysts.label: Late-breaking LEVEL data presentation at ESC Congress 2026
catalysts.detail: Full LEVEL trial results will be presented in a Late-Breaking Clinical Science session at the European Society of Cardiology Congress in Munich (Aug 28-31), presented by Prof. Sanjiv Shah, MD, Northwestern University, on Saturday Aug 29.

catalysts.label: LEVEL-2 global Phase 3 enrollment completion
catalysts.detail: The second registrational Phase 3 study (LEVEL-2, ~540 patients across 100+ sites in 15 countries) is expected to complete enrollment by the end of 2027.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 financial results
catalysts.detail: Tenax's next quarterly earnings report and corporate update is estimated for mid-August 2026 by third-party earnings trackers (Q1 2026 results were released May 12), likely alongside or shortly after the LEVEL topline data disclosure; the company has not yet issued an official press release confirming the exact date.

pulse: As of July 22, 2026, Tenax Therapeutics remains in a quiet holding pattern ahead of the pivotal readout for oral levosimendan (TNX-103) in pulmonary hypertension due to HFpEF, with topline Phase 3 LEVEL data expected in August 2026 and a late-breaking full data presentation confirmed for ESC Congress in Munich on August 29. Institutional positioning has grown more crowded and two-sided into the catalyst: Fidelity's FMR LLC disclosed a fresh 14.9% stake and T. Rowe Price reported a 17.2% stake as of June 30, even as Millennium Management trimmed its stake to 3.3% from 5.1%, while short interest simultaneously surged 132.9% to about 7% of float, reflecting both bullish accumulation and rising bearish/hedging bets ahead of the binary event. The company enters the readout on solid financial footing (cash of $118.8 million as of March 31, 2026, funding operations into at least Q1 2028) and with strengthened market positioning: a long-term manufacturing/supply deal with Orion (regulatory deadline extended to 2035) and a bullish sell-side consensus ("Moderate Buy"/"Strong Buy", average price targets in the mid-$30s, with Piper Sandler's target as high as $50 and Guggenheim at $40). The stock's near-term trajectory hinges almost entirely on whether LEVEL hits its 6-minute-walk-distance primary endpoint, a binary catalyst that would position TNX-103 as a potential first-in-class approved PH-HFpEF therapy; Q2 2026 earnings are unofficially estimated around mid-August but have not yet been formally scheduled by the company.
